title: Cut a BigQuery query's cost with dry-runs and the query plan
description: Measure bytes-scanned with --dry_run, read the execution plan stages, then add pruning, clustering, or materialization.
body: Use when a BigQuery query is expensive or slow. Do not guess at fixes. Actions: run the query with --dry_run to capture bytes that would be billed, inspect the query plan stages for slot-heavy shuffles and full scans, then apply targeted fixes — add a partition filter, restrict columns, pre-aggregate into a materialized view, or cluster the source. Re-run the dry-run to confirm the drop. Validation signals include dry-run bytes-billed falling, plan shuffle bytes shrinking, and runtime dropping at the same slot reservation. Risk boundary: changing a production dataset's schema or partitioning is human-gated.
